message:
Merge PR #2633: feat: event-driven polecat lifecycle with FIX_NEEDED feedback loop

Adds awaiting_verdict state and FIX_NEEDED protocol for refinery-to-polecat
feedback on merge failures, replacing the old reopen-and-wait approach.

PR: https://github.com/steveyegge/gastown/pull/2633
Author: ldangelo

files.patch:
@@ -5,30 +5,46 @@ This molecule guides a polecat through a complete work assignment. Each step
 has clear entry/exit criteria and specific commands to run. A polecat can
 crash after any step and resume from the last completed step.
 
-## Polecat Contract (Self-Cleaning Model)
+## Polecat Contract (Event-Driven Lifecycle)
 
-You are a self-cleaning worker. You:
+You are an event-driven worker. You:
 1. Receive work via your hook (formula checklist + issue)
 2. Work through formula steps in order (shown inline at prime time)
-3. Complete and self-clean via `gt done` (submit + nuke yourself)
-4. You are GONE - Refinery merges from MQ
+3. Submit your MR and enter awaiting_verdict state
+4. Refinery processes your MR:
+   - MERGED → you self-clean via `gt done` (gone)
+   - FIX_NEEDED → you read failure, fix code, resubmit (loop)
+5. You only die when refinery confirms merge
 
-**Self-cleaning:** When you run `gt done`, you push your work, submit to MQ,
-nuke your sandbox, and exit. There is no idle state. Done means gone.
+**Event-driven:** After submitting your MR, you stay alive waiting for the
+refinery verdict. If the merge fails, you fix the code in-place without
+losing context. Only on MERGED do you self-clean.
 
 **Important:** This formula defines the workflow template. Steps are shown inline
 when you run `gt prime` — there are no separate step beads to close. Work through
-the checklist, then run `gt done`.
+the checklist, await the verdict, then run `gt done` when merged.
 
 **Speed principle:** You run the full gate suite AFTER rebasing onto the target
 branch (pre-verify step). This enables the refinery to fast-path merge your MR
 in ~5 seconds instead of re-running gates. If pre-verification is skipped or
 stale, the refinery falls through to normal gate execution.
 
+**Lifecycle:**
+```
+spawning → working → mr_submitted → awaiting_verdict
+                                          │
+                          ┌───────────────┴───────────────┐
+                          ▼                               ▼
+                    FIX_NEEDED                          MERGED
+                          │                               │
+                    (fix & resubmit)                  (gt done → gone)
+                          │
+                    awaiting_verdict (loop)
+```
+
 **You do NOT:**
 - Push directly to main (Refinery merges from MQ)
 - Close your own issue (Refinery closes after merge)
-- Wait for merge (you're gone after `gt done`)
 - Fix pre-existing failures on main (Refinery owns main health)
 
 ## Variables
@@ -48,9 +64,11 @@ stale, the refinery falls through to normal gate execution.
 | Situation | Action |
 |-----------|--------|
 | Build fails | Fix it. Do not proceed if it won't compile. |
+| FIX_NEEDED from refinery | Read failure details, fix code, resubmit |
 | Blocked on external | Mail Witness for help, mark yourself stuck |
 | Context filling | Use gt handoff to cycle to fresh session |
-| Unsure what to do | Mail Witness, don't guess |"""
+| Unsure what to do | Mail Witness, don't guess |
+| Max fix attempts exceeded | Mail Witness, enter stuck state |"""
 formula = "mol-polecat-work"
 version = 9
 
@@ -464,36 +482,156 @@ The refinery will run gates normally (slower but still correct).
 **Exit criteria:** Branch rebased onto latest origin/{{base_branch}}, all configured gates pass."""
 
 [[steps]]
-id = "submit-and-exit"
-title = "Submit work and self-clean"
+id = "submit-mr"
+title = "Submit MR and enter awaiting_verdict"
 needs = ["pre-verify"]
 description = """
-Submit your work and clean up. You cease to exist after this step.
-
-**Self-Cleaning Model:**
-Once you run `gt done`, you're gone. The command:
-1. Pushes your branch to origin
-2. Creates an MR bead in the merge queue
-3. Nukes your sandbox (worktree removal)
-4. Exits your session immediately
+Submit your MR to the merge queue but stay alive for the verdict.
 
 **Pre-flight: Verify you have actual work to submit (HARD GATE):**
 ```bash
 git log origin/{{base_branch}}..HEAD --oneline
 ```
-This MUST show at least 1 commit. If it shows nothing, do NOT run `gt done`.
-`gt done` will reject zero-commit branches for polecats.
+This MUST show at least 1 commit. If it shows nothing, do NOT submit.
+
+**Report-only tasks (no commits — audits, reviews, research):**
+If this is a report-only task with findings persisted to the bead,
+run `gt done --cleanup-status clean` and skip remaining steps.
+
+**Push and submit MR:**
+```bash
+git push origin HEAD
+
+# Submit to merge queue (creates MR bead, does NOT nuke sandbox)
+gt mq submit {{issue}}
+```
+
+Track the MR bead ID from the submit output — you need it for resubmission.
+
+**Enter awaiting_verdict state:**
+After submission, you are in the `awaiting_verdict` state. The refinery will
+process your MR and send you one of:
+- **MERGED**: Your work was merged successfully → proceed to self-clean step
+- **FIX_NEEDED**: Tests/build/lint failed → proceed to await-verdict step
+
+**Exit criteria:** MR submitted to merge queue, polecat in awaiting_verdict state."""
+
+[[steps]]
+id = "await-verdict"
+title = "Wait for refinery verdict"
+needs = ["submit-mr"]
+description = """
+Wait for the refinery to process your MR and send you a verdict.
+
+**Check for verdict signal:**
+
+First check your mail for FIX_NEEDED or MERGED messages:
+```bash
+gt mail inbox
+```
+
+If no verdict yet, use await-signal to wait:
+```bash
+gt mol step await-signal --agent-bead <your-agent-bead> \
+  --backoff-base 30s --backoff-mult 2 --backoff-max 5m
+```
+
+**On MERGED signal:**
+The refinery merged your work successfully. Proceed to self-clean step.
+
+**On FIX_NEEDED signal:**
+The refinery found failures in your MR. The FIX_NEEDED message contains:
+- Failure-Type: tests, build, lint, typecheck
+- Error: the actual error output
+- Attempt-Number: how many fix attempts so far
+
+**Fix procedure (on FIX_NEEDED):**
+
+1. **Read the failure details** from your mail:
+```bash
+gt mail inbox
+# Read the FIX_NEEDED message for failure details
+```
+
+2. **Also check the bead for updated failure notes:**
+```bash
+bd show {{issue}}
+```
+
+3. **Fix the code:**
+   - Diagnose the failure based on the error output
+   - Make targeted fixes (don't refactor unrelated code)
+   - Commit your fix:
+     ```bash
+     git add <files>
+     git commit -m "fix: address <failure-type> failure ({{issue}})"
+     ```
+
+4. **Re-run the failing checks locally:**
+   - If failure was tests: run `{{test_command}}`
+   - If failure was build: run `{{build_command}}`
+   - If failure was lint: run `{{lint_command}}`
+   - If failure was typecheck: run `{{typecheck_command}}`
+
+5. **Rebase and push:**
+```bash
+git fetch origin {{base_branch}}
+git rebase origin/{{base_branch}}
+git push origin HEAD --force-with-lease
+```
+
+6. **Resubmit to merge queue:**
+```bash
+gt mq submit {{issue}} --resubmit
+```
+
+7. **Loop back to awaiting_verdict** — wait for the next verdict.
+
+**Max retry limit:** If Attempt-Number >= 3, do NOT keep retrying.
+Instead, escalate:
+```bash
+gt mail send <rig>/witness -s "HELP: Cannot fix merge failure after 3 attempts" \
+  --stdin <<'BODY'
+Issue: {{issue}}
+Failure-Type: <type>
+Error: <error>
+Attempts: 3
+I've tried to fix this failure 3 times and cannot resolve it.
+Please advise or reassign.
+BODY
+```
+
+**If your session is dying (context filling):**
+Use handoff to preserve your state:
+```bash
+gt handoff -s "Awaiting verdict for {{issue}}" -m "MR submitted, waiting for refinery.
+Attempt: <N>
+Last failure: <type if any>"
+```
+
+**Exit criteria:** Received MERGED signal from refinery."""
+
+[[steps]]
+id = "self-clean"
+title = "Self-clean after merge confirmation"
+needs = ["await-verdict"]
+description = """
+The refinery confirmed your work was merged. Self-clean and exit.
+
+**Verify MERGED signal:**
+Confirm you received a MERGED signal (not just timing out):
+```bash
+gt mail inbox
+# Should show MERGED message for your branch
+```
 
 **Run gt done:**
 ```bash
-# For code tasks with pre-verification (recommended — enables fast-path merge):
+# With pre-verification (if you ran gates before submitting):
 gt done --pre-verified
 
-# For code tasks without pre-verification (gates skipped or failed):
+# Without pre-verification:
 gt done
-
-# For report-only tasks (no commits — audits, reviews, research):
-gt done --cleanup-status clean
 ```
 
 You should see output like:
@@ -507,16 +645,10 @@ You should see output like:
 ✓ Session exiting
 ```
 
-**What happens next (not your concern):**
-- Refinery batches MRs and runs gates on the merged stack
-- If green: all MRs in the batch merge to {{base_branch}} at once
-- If red: Refinery bisects to isolate the culprit, merges the good ones
-- Refinery fixes failures itself (inline or via helper polecats)
-- Refinery closes your issue after successful merge
-
-You are NOT involved in any of that. You're gone. Done means gone.
+**You are GONE after this.** The refinery has already merged your work.
+`gt done` at this point is just cleanup (nuke sandbox, close session).
 
-**Exit criteria:** Work submitted, sandbox nuked, session exited."""
+**Exit criteria:** Sandbox nuked, session exited."""
 
 [vars]
 [vars.issue]

@@ -10,31 +10,36 @@ The Refinery is the Engineer in the engine room. You process polecat branches, m
 The Refinery receives MERGE_READY mail from Witnesses when polecats complete work:
 
 ```
-Witness                    Refinery                      Git
-   │                          │                           │
-   │ MERGE_READY              │                           │
-   │─────────────────────────>│                           │
-   │                          │                           │
-   │                    (verify branch)                   │
-   │                          │ fetch & rebase            │
-   │                          │──────────────────────────>│
-   │                          │                           │
-   │                    (run tests)                       │
-   │                          │                           │
-   │                    (quality review, if enabled)      │
-   │                          │                           │
-   │                    (if pass)                         │
-   │                          │ merge & push              │
-   │                          │──────────────────────────>│
-   │                          │                           │
-   │ MERGED                   │                           │
-   │<─────────────────────────│                           │
-   │                          │                           │
+Witness                    Refinery                      Git        Polecat
+   │                          │                           │            │
+   │ MERGE_READY              │                           │            │
+   │─────────────────────────>│                           │            │
+   │                          │                           │            │
+   │                    (verify branch)                   │            │
+   │                          │ fetch & rebase            │            │
+   │                          │──────────────────────────>│            │
+   │                          │                           │            │
+   │                    (run tests)                       │            │
+   │                          │                           │            │
+   │                    (if pass)                         │            │
+   │                          │ merge & push              │            │
+   │                          │──────────────────────────>│            │
+   │ MERGED                   │                           │            │
+   │<─────────────────────────│                           │            │
+   │                          │                           │            │
+   │                    (if fail)                         │            │
+   │                          │          FIX_NEEDED       │            │
+   │                          │───────────────────────────────────────>│
+   │                          │                           │   (fix code)
+   │                          │                           │  (resubmit)
 ```
 
 After successful merge, Refinery sends MERGED mail back to Witness so it can
 complete cleanup (nuke the polecat worktree).
 
+On failure, Refinery sends FIX_NEEDED directly to the Polecat. The polecat
+fixes the code in-place and resubmits the MR without losing context.
+
 ## CRITICAL: Read Step Instructions Before Acting
 
 Before executing ANY step, you MUST run `bd show <step-id>` and read the full
@@ -48,23 +53,19 @@ source of truth.
 
 | Variable | Default | Description |
 |----------|---------|-------------|
-| wisp_type | {{wisp_type}} | Type of wisp created for this molecule |
-| integration_branch_refinery_enabled | {{integration_branch_refinery_enabled}} | Whether refinery merges to integration branches |
-| integration_branch_auto_land | {{integration_branch_auto_land}} | Whether to auto-land integration branches when epic children all closed |
-| run_tests | {{run_tests}} | Whether to run tests before merging |
-| setup_command | {{setup_command}} | Setup/install command (e.g., `pnpm install`). Empty = skip. |
-| typecheck_command | {{typecheck_command}} | Type check command (e.g., `tsc --noEmit`). Empty = skip. |
-| lint_command | {{lint_command}} | Lint command (e.g., `eslint .`). Empty = skip. |
-| test_command | {{test_command}} | Test command to run (if run_tests is true) |
-| build_command | {{build_command}} | Build command (e.g., `go build ./...`). Empty = skip. |
-| target_branch | {{target_branch}} | Default target branch for merges |
-| delete_merged_branches | {{delete_merged_branches}} | Whether to delete source branches after merge |
-| judgment_enabled | {{judgment_enabled}} | Enable quality review for merges (true/false) |
-| review_depth | {{review_depth}} | Review depth: quick, standard, or deep |
-| review_enabled | {{review_enabled}} | Enable /review code review gating (replaces judgment_enabled) |
-| review_blocking | {{review_blocking}} | Whether grade C/D blocks merges (triggers FIX_NEEDED) |
-| review_pass_grade | {{review_pass_grade}} | Minimum passing grade (A, B, C, or D) |
-| review_max_retries | {{review_max_retries}} | Max times to bounce back to polecat for review fixes |
+| wisp_type | patrol | Type of wisp created for this molecule |
+| integration_branch_refinery_enabled | true | Whether refinery merges to integration branches |
+| integration_branch_auto_land | false | Whether to auto-land integration branches when epic children all closed |
+| run_tests | true | Whether to run tests before merging |
+| setup_command | (empty) | Setup/install command (e.g., `pnpm install`). Empty = skip. |
+| typecheck_command | (empty) | Type check command (e.g., `tsc --noEmit`). Empty = skip. |
+| lint_command | (empty) | Lint command (e.g., `eslint .`). Empty = skip. |
+| test_command | go test ./... | Test command to run (if run_tests is true) |
+| build_command | (empty) | Build command (e.g., `go build ./...`). Empty = skip. |
+| target_branch | main | Default target branch for merges |
+| delete_merged_branches | true | Whether to delete source branches after merge |
+| judgment_enabled | false | Enable quality review for merges (true/false) |
+| review_depth | standard | Review depth: quick, standard, or deep |
 
 ## Target Resolution Rule
 
@@ -85,7 +86,7 @@ resolve that placeholder with this rule:
 You MUST process steps in strict DAG order. Walk through each step sequentially,
 unless you are explicitly told to skip to a step."""
 formula = "mol-refinery-patrol"
-version = 10
+version = 11
 
 [vars]
 [vars.wisp_type]
@@ -117,8 +118,8 @@ description = "Lint command (e.g., eslint .). Empty = skip."
 default = ""
 
 [vars.test_command]
-description = "Test command to run (if run_tests is true). Auto-detected from rig settings."
-default = ""
+description = "Test command to run (if run_tests is true)"
+default = "go test ./..."
 
 [vars.build_command]
 description = "Build command (e.g., go build ./...). Empty = skip."
@@ -140,22 +141,6 @@ default = "false"
 description = "Review depth: quick, standard, or deep"
 default = "standard"
 
-[vars.review_enabled]
-description = "Enable /review code review gating for merges (true/false). Takes priority over judgment_enabled when true."
-default = "false"
-
-[vars.review_blocking]
-description = "Whether grade C/D triggers FIX_NEEDED in refinery (vs just logging findings)"
-default = "false"
-
-[vars.review_pass_grade]
-description = "Minimum passing grade in refinery: A, B, C, or D (default: B)"
-default = "B"
-
-[vars.review_max_retries]
-description = "Max times to bounce back to polecat for review fixes before proceeding"
-default = "3"
-
 [[steps]]
 id = "inbox-check"
 title = "Check refinery mail"
@@ -393,234 +378,117 @@ Track results: pass count, fail count, specific failures."""
 
 [[steps]]
 id = "quality-review"
-title = "Code review merge diff"
+title = "Quality review merge diff"
 needs = ["run-tests"]
 description = """
-**Config: review_enabled = {{review_enabled}}**
 **Config: judgment_enabled = {{judgment_enabled}}**
 **Config: review_depth = {{review_depth}}**
-**Config: review_blocking = {{review_blocking}}**
-**Config: review_pass_grade = {{review_pass_grade}}**
-**Config: review_max_retries = {{review_max_retries}}**
 
-Review the merge diff using /review grading (A-F with CRITICAL/MAJOR/MINOR).
+Review the merge diff for quality issues. This step is measurement-only (Phase 1):
+reviews are recorded but do NOT gate merges.
 
-**Step 1: Check if review is enabled**
+**Step 1: Check if quality review is enabled**
 
-If review_enabled is "true": use the new grading system (this step).
-Else if judgment_enabled is "true": fall back to legacy scoring (0.0-1.0).
-Else: skip this step entirely. Log: "Code review skipped (review_enabled=false)"
+If judgment_enabled is not "true", skip this step entirely and proceed to
+handle-failures. Log: "Quality review skipped (judgment_enabled=false)"
 
 **Step 2: Get the merge diff**
 
-Determine `<review-target>` using the **Target Resolution Rule** above.
-
 ```bash
-git diff origin/<review-target>...temp
+git diff origin/main...temp
 ```
 
 If the diff is empty, skip with: "No diff to review"
 
-**Step 3: Review the diff (CRITICAL/MAJOR/MINOR grading)**
-
-Review the diff yourself. For each issue found, classify by severity:
+**Step 3: Review the diff**
 
-**CRITICAL** - Must fix before merge:
-- Security vulnerabilities (injection, auth bypass, secrets in code)
-- Data loss risks (missing transactions, unsafe deletes)
-- Correctness bugs (race conditions, nil dereference, logic errors)
+Review the diff yourself using your judgment. Assess:
 
-**MAJOR** - Should fix before merge:
-- Logic errors that may not crash but produce wrong results
-- Missing error handling on external calls
-- API contract violations
-- Missing tests for critical paths
-
-**MINOR** - Nice to fix:
-- Style inconsistencies with surrounding code
-- Naming issues (unclear or misleading names)
-- Missing comments on non-obvious logic
-- Minor code smells
+1. **Correctness**: Logic errors, off-by-one, nil/null handling, race conditions
+2. **Security**: Injection, auth bypass, secrets in code, unsafe deserialization
+3. **Clarity**: Naming, structure, comments where non-obvious
+4. **Style**: Consistency with surrounding code, idiomatic patterns
 
 Depth is controlled by review_depth:
-- `quick`: Focus on CRITICAL only. Skim for security and correctness.
-- `standard`: All severities. Moderate detail.
+- `quick`: Focus on correctness and security only. Skim for obvious issues.
+- `standard`: All four categories. Moderate detail.
 - `deep`: Thorough line-by-line review. Flag even minor style issues.
 
-For each issue, note:
-- File and line number
-- Severity (CRITICAL, MAJOR, MINOR)
-- Description of the issue
-- Suggested fix (specific, actionable)
-
-**Step 4: Assign grade**
+**Step 4: Score and recommend**
 
-Grade is determined by the highest severity issue found:
+Assign a score from 0.0 to 1.0:
+- 0.9-1.0: Excellent — no issues or only trivial nits
+- 0.7-0.89: Good — minor issues only
+- 0.45-0.69: Needs work — significant issues found
+- 0.0-0.44: Breach — critical issues (security, correctness)
 
-| Grade | Criteria | Verdict |
-|-------|----------|---------|
-| **A** | No CRITICAL, MAJOR, or MINOR issues | PASS |
-| **B** | MINOR issues only (no CRITICAL or MAJOR) | PASS |
-| **C** | MAJOR issues present (no CRITICAL) | FAIL |
-| **D** | CRITICAL issues present | FAIL |
-| **F** | Unreviewable (empty diff, binary files, generated code only) | SKIP |
+Recommendation:
+- `approve`: Score >= 0.45
+- `request_changes`: Score < 0.45
 
 **Step 5: Record the result as a wisp**
 
-Map grade to legacy score for trend tracking compatibility:
-- A → 1.0, B → 0.8, C → 0.55, D → 0.3, F → 0.0
+Record the quality review result. This is fail-open: if the command fails,
+log the error and continue — never block the merge.
 
 ```bash
-bd create "code-review: Grade <grade>, <PASS|FAIL>" -t chore --ephemeral \\
-  -l type:plugin-run,plugin:quality-review-result,worker:<polecat-name>,rig:<rig-name>,score:<mapped-score>,grade:<grade>,recommendation:<approve|request_changes>,result:success \\
-  -d "Grade: <grade>. CRITICAL: <count>, MAJOR: <count>, MINOR: <count>. Verdict: <PASS|FAIL>" \\
+bd create "quality-review: Score <score>, <recommendation>" -t chore --ephemeral \\
+  -l type:plugin-run,plugin:quality-review-result,worker:<polecat-name>,rig:<rig-name>,score:<score>,recommendation:<approve|request_changes>,result:success \\
+  -d "Score: <score>, <recommendation>. Issues: <count> (<summary>)" \\
   --silent 2>/dev/null || true
 ```
 
-**Step 6: Determine merge/block action**
-
-Compare the assigned grade against review_pass_grade (default: B):
-- Grade meets or exceeds pass grade → PASS (proceed to handle-failures)
-- Grade below pass grade → review fails
-
-If review fails AND review_blocking is "true":
-- Set review_failed = true (used by handle-failures)
-- Store the full structured review output:
-  ```
-  Grade: <grade>
-
-  CRITICAL (<count> issues)
-    <file>:<line> — <description>
-      Suggested fix: <actionable fix>
+**Step 6: Handle breach scores (measurement-only)**
 
-  MAJOR (<count> issues)
-    <file>:<line> — <description>
-      Suggested fix: <actionable fix>
+If score < 0.45 (breach threshold):
+- Add a note to your merge summary: "Warning: Quality review breach (score: X.XX)"
+- List the critical/major issues found
+- Do NOT block the merge — Phase 1 is measurement-only
+- Proceed to handle-failures normally
 
-  MINOR (<count> issues)
-    <file>:<line> — <description>
-      Suggested fix: <actionable fix>
-
-  Summary: <N> CRITICAL, <N> MAJOR, <N> MINOR
-  Verdict: FAIL
-  ```
-- Proceed to handle-failures (which will send FIX_NEEDED)
-
-If review fails AND review_blocking is "false":
-- Log: "Warning: Code review failed (Grade: <grade>) but review_blocking=false, proceeding"
-- List the issues found for the merge summary
-- Proceed to handle-failures normally (no block)
-
-Grade A or B always passes regardless of review_blocking.
-
-Track: grade, verdict, issue counts by severity, review duration."""
+Track: review score, recommendation, issue count, duration."""
 
 [[steps]]
 id = "handle-failures"
-title = "Handle quality check, test, or code review failures"
+title = "Handle quality check or test failures"
 needs = ["quality-review"]
 description = """
 **VERIFICATION GATE**: This step enforces the Beads Promise.
-**Config: review_blocking = {{review_blocking}}**
-**Config: review_max_retries = {{review_max_retries}}**
-
-If all checks, tests, AND code review PASSED: This step auto-completes. Proceed to merge.
-
-## Code Review Failure (FailureType: code-review)
-
-If quality-review step set review_failed = true (grade below pass grade AND review_blocking = true):
-
-1. **Log detailed findings on the bead** so the polecat can read them:
-   ```bash
-   bd update <issue-id> --notes "Code review findings (Grade: <grade>):
-
-   <full structured review output from quality-review step>
-
-   Fix the issues above and resubmit with 'gt done'."
-   ```
-
-2. **Check retry count**: Check the MR bead for a `review_retries` label.
-   - If review_retries >= review_max_retries: Log "Max review retries reached, proceeding with merge"
-     and skip the FIX_NEEDED flow. Proceed to merge-push.
-   - Otherwise: increment retry count and continue with FIX_NEEDED.
-
-3. **Reopen the source issue** for rework:
-   ```bash
-   bd update <issue-id> --status=open --assignee=""
-   bd sync
-   ```
-
-4. **Send MERGE_FAILED with code-review details** to witness:
-   ```bash
-   gt mail send <rig>/witness -s "MERGE_FAILED <polecat-name>" --stdin <<'BODY'
-   Branch: <branch>
-   Issue: <issue-id>
-   Polecat: <polecat-name>
-   Rig: <rig>
-   FailureType: code-review
-   Grade: <grade>
-   Error: Code review failed (Grade: <grade>). <N> CRITICAL, <N> MAJOR, <N> MINOR issues found.
-
-   Review findings:
-   <full structured review output with file:line and suggested fixes>
-   BODY
-   ```
-
-5. **Close the MR bead** with review failure reason:
-   ```bash
-   bd close <mr-bead-id> --reason "Code review failed (Grade: <grade>): <N> CRITICAL, <N> MAJOR, <N> MINOR"
-   ```
-
-6. **Do NOT delete the branch** — the polecat needs it to apply fixes.
-   (Unlike test/build failures where a fresh branch is created, code review
-   failures preserve the branch for incremental fixes.)
-
-7. Archive the MERGE_READY message and skip to loop-check.
-

 description = "Per-rig worker monitor patrol loop.

The Witness is the Pit Boss for your rig. You watch polecats, nudge them toward
completion, verify clean git state before kills, and escalate stuck workers.

**You do NOT do implementation work.** Your job is oversight, not coding.

## Persistent Polecat Model (gt-4ac)

Polecats persist after work completion — sandbox is preserved for reuse:

```
Polecat lifecycle: spawning → working → mr_submitted → idle (sandbox preserved)
MR lifecycle:      created → queued → processed → merged (Refinery handles)
```

Once a polecat calls gt done and submits an MR, it transitions to idle state.
The MR lifecycle continues independently in the Refinery. The polecat is NOT
nuked — its sandbox is preserved for reuse by future slings.

**CRITICAL**: Do NOT nuke polecats with pending MRs. The refinery needs the
remote branch to exist to process the merge. Nuking deletes the remote branch
and orphans the MR. See gt-6a9d.

**Key principle**: Polecat lifecycle is separate from MR lifecycle. Polecats
go idle after work, they are NOT destroyed.

## Restart-First Policy (gt-dsgp)

The witness NEVER nukes polecats automatically. When a polecat is stuck, hung,
or has a dead agent process, the witness RESTARTS the session instead of nuking.
This preserves the polecat's worktree and branch, preventing work loss.

- Dead agent process → restart session
- Hung session (no output 30+ min) → restart session
- Stuck in gt done → restart session
- Done polecat (bead closed) → leave alone (sandbox preserved)
- Polecat with pending MR → leave alone (refinery handles)

Nuking only happens via explicit `gt polecat nuke` command from a human or Mayor.

## Design Philosophy

This patrol follows Gas Town principles:
- **Discovery over tracking**: Observe reality each cycle, with minimal agent-bead state for duration tracking
- **Beads over mail**: survey-workers discovers completion state from agent bead metadata (gt-w0br); inbox-check POLECAT_DONE is fallback only
- **Persistent by default**: Clean polecats go idle, sandbox preserved for reuse (gt-4ac)
- **Cleanup wisps for merge tracking**: Created when MR is pending in refinery
- **Task tool for parallelism**: Subagents inspect polecats, not molecule arms
- **Swim lane discipline**: Only close wisps YOU created. Wisp lifecycle for non-witness wisps is the reaper Dog's job. Report orphaned foreign wisps — never close them.

## Patrol Shape (Linear)

```
inbox-check ─► process-cleanups ─► check-refinery ─► survey-workers
                                                            │
         ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
         ▼
  check-timer-gates ─► check-swarm ─► patrol-cleanup ─► context-check ─► loop-or-exit
```

No dynamic arms. No fanout gates. No persistent nudge counters.
State is discovered each cycle from reality (tmux, beads, mail)."

 [vars.wisp_type]

 [[steps]]
-description = "Survey all polecats using agent beads and tmux session cross-reference.

## PRIMARY: Discover completions from agent bead metadata (gt-w0br)

Before zombie detection or progress checks, scan agent beads for completion
metadata written by `gt done`. This is the PRIMARY mechanism for discovering
polecat state transitions. The inbox-check POLECAT_DONE mail is now fallback only.

Completion metadata fields on agent beads (set by gt done):
- `exit_type`: COMPLETED, ESCALATED, DEFERRED, PHASE_COMPLETE
- `mr_id`: MR bead ID (if MR was created)
- `branch`: Working branch name
- `mr_failed`: true if MR creation failed
- `completion_time`: RFC3339 timestamp

**Step 0: Discover completions from beads**

The `DiscoverCompletions()` function (witness/handlers.go) handles this:
1. Scans all polecat agent beads for `exit_type` + `completion_time` set
2. Routes each: MR present → cleanup wisp + MERGE_READY; no MR → acknowledge idle
3. Clears completion metadata after processing (prevents re-processing)

This replaces the reactive POLECAT_DONE mail flow with proactive bead discovery.

🚨 **SWIM LANE RULE: You may ONLY close wisps that YOU (the witness) created.**
Do NOT close formula wisps, polecat work wisps, or any wisp created by `gt sling`
or another agent. Wisp lifecycle for non-witness wisps is the reaper Dog's job.
If you encounter wisps that look orphaned but weren't created by your patrol,
report them to Deacon — do NOT close them. Closing foreign wisps kills active
polecat work molecules.

**Step 1: List polecat agent beads**

```bash
bd list --type=agent --json
```

Filter the JSON output for entries where description contains `role_type: polecat`.
Each polecat agent bead has fields in its description:
- `role_type: polecat`
- `rig: <rig-name>`
- `agent_state: running|idle|stuck|done`
- `hook_bead: <current-work-id>`

**Step 2: For each polecat, check agent_state**

| agent_state | Meaning | Action |
|-------------|---------|--------|
| running | Actively working | Check for zombie (Step 2a), then progress (Step 3) |
| working | Actively working | Check for zombie (Step 2a), then progress (Step 3) |
| spawning | Agent initializing | Skip zombie detection. Check spawn age (Step 2b) |
| idle | No work assigned | Leave alone — sandbox preserved for reuse (Step 3a) |
| stuck | Self-reported stuck | Handle stuck protocol |
| done | Work complete | Verify cleanup triggered (see Step 4a) |

**Step 2a: ZOMBIE DETECTION — Cross-reference tmux session existence**

🚨 **CRITICAL**: Zombies cannot send signals. A polecat with agent_state=running
or hook_bead assigned but NO tmux session is a zombie that will sit forever
undetected unless you proactively check.

⚠️ **SKIP spawning polecats**: Polecats with agent_state=spawning are still
initializing (worktree creation, dependency install, tmux session startup).
They will NOT have a tmux session yet — this is expected, not a zombie.
Do NOT run zombie detection on spawning polecats. Handle them in Step 2b instead.

For EVERY polecat with agent_state=running/working (NOT spawning) OR hook_bead assigned with non-spawning state:
```bash
gt session status <rig>/<name> --json | jq -r '.running' | grep -q true && echo ALIVE || echo ZOMBIE
```

**If ZOMBIE detected** (session missing, agent says working):

**IMPORTANT (gt-sy8)**: Before processing as zombie, check if the hook_bead is
already CLOSED:
```bash
bd show <hook_bead> --json | jq -r '.[0].status'
```
If status is "closed", the polecat completed its work successfully. The dead
session is expected (gt done kills it). Just nuke the dead session — do NOT
trigger re-dispatch or send RECOVERED_BEAD/RECOVERY_NEEDED to Deacon.

1. Check git state to determine if work is recoverable:
```bash
cd polecats/<name>/<rig>
git status --porcelain         # Uncommitted changes?
git log @{u}..HEAD      # Unpushed commits?
```

2. **If clean** (no uncommitted, no unpushed): Check for pending MR first.
```bash
# CRITICAL (gt-6a9d): Check for pending MR before any nuke!
bd list --label polecat:<name>,state:merge-requested --status=open
# If merge-requested wisp exists → DO NOT NUKE, MR pending in refinery
# If no pending MR → safe to nuke (zombie with no work to preserve)
gt session restart <rig>/<name>
```

3. **If dirty** (has unpushed/uncommitted work): Escalate to Deacon for recovery.
```bash
gt mail send deacon/ -s "RECOVERY_NEEDED <rig>/<name>" \
  -m "Polecat: <rig>/<name>
Cleanup Status: <has_uncommitted|has_unpushed|has_stash>
Hook Bead: <hook_bead>
Detected: $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)

Zombie detected: tmux session dead, agent_state=<state>.
This polecat has unpushed/uncommitted work that will be lost if nuked.
Please coordinate recovery before authorizing cleanup."
```

Also create a cleanup wisp for tracking:
```bash
bd create --ephemeral --title "cleanup:<name>" \
  --description "Zombie detected: session dead, state=<agent_state>" \
  --labels cleanup,polecat:<name>,state:zombie-detected
```

**Step 2b: STALE SPAWN DETECTION — Check spawn age for spawning polecats**

For polecats with agent_state=spawning, check how long they've been spawning.
Spawning should complete within 5 minutes even on large repos.

```bash
# Get the agent bead's updated_at timestamp to estimate spawn start
bd show <agent-bead> --json | jq -r '.[0].updated_at'
# Compare with current time
```

| Spawn age | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| < 5 min | Normal — leave alone, spawning in progress |
| 5-10 min | Warning — log observation, check again next cycle |
| > 10 min | Stale spawn — escalate (do NOT nuke) |

**If stale spawn detected** (spawning > 10 min):
```bash
gt escalate -s HIGH "Stale spawn: <rig>/<name> has been spawning for <N> minutes"
```

Do NOT nuke stale spawning polecats. The sling process may be slow (large repo
clone, dependency install) or stuck. Escalation lets a human or Mayor investigate
without destroying a potentially-in-progress setup.

**Step 3: For running polecats (with LIVE session), assess progress**

Check the hook_bead field to see what they're working on:
```bash
bd show <hook_bead>  # See current step/issue
```

You can also verify they're responsive:
```bash
gt peek <rig>/<name> 20
```

Look for:
- Recent tool activity → making progress
- Idle at prompt → may need nudge
- Error messages → may need help

**Step 3a: For idle polecats, verify sandbox health**

When agent_state=idle, the polecat has no work assigned. Its sandbox is
preserved for reuse by future slings (persistent polecat model, gt-4ac).

⚠️ **Do NOT nuke idle polecats.** Their sandbox is preserved for reuse.
Nuking would force a full re-clone on the next sling, which is slow.

Check for pending MRs — an idle polecat may have work in the refinery:
```bash
# Check for cleanup wisps (merge-requested = MR pending in refinery)
bd list --label polecat:<name>,state:merge-requested --status=open
```
If a merge-requested wisp exists, the polecat's MR is in the refinery queue.
Do NOT nuke — the refinery needs the remote branch.

**If dirty** (uncommitted or unpushed work):
```bash
# Escalate to Deacon - polecat has work that might be valuable
gt mail send deacon/ -s \"IDLE_DIRTY: <polecat> has uncommitted work\" \
  -m \"Polecat: <name>
State: idle (no hook_bead)
Git status: <uncommitted-files>
Unpushed commits: <count>

Please advise: recover work or discard?\"
```

**Rationale**: Idle polecats are preserved for reuse. Their sandbox contains
a pre-configured worktree that saves clone time on the next sling. Only
escalate when there's actual dirty state at risk.

**Step 4: Decide action**

| Observation | Action |
|-------------|--------|
| agent_state=running, session alive, recent activity | None |
| agent_state=running, session alive, idle 5-15 min | Gentle nudge |
| agent_state=running, session alive, idle 15+ min | Direct nudge with deadline |
| agent_state=running, SESSION DEAD | ZOMBIE — handle in Step 2a |
| agent_state=spawning, < 5 min | None — spawning in progress |
| agent_state=spawning, 5-10 min | Log warning, check next cycle |
| agent_state=spawning, > 10 min | Stale spawn — escalate (Step 2b) |
| agent_state=stuck | Assess and help or escalate |
| agent_state=done | Verify cleanup triggered (see Step 4a) |

**Step 4a: Handle agent_state=done**

In the persistent model, polecats with agent_state=done should be idle with
their sandbox preserved. Finding one here indicates:

1. **Stale agent bead** - polecat was nuked but bead remains
   ```bash
   # Verify polecat doesn't exist anymore
   ls polecats/<name> 2>/dev/null || echo "Already nuked"
   ```
   If nuked, the agent bead is stale. Clean it up or ignore.

2. **Cleanup wisp exists** - polecat has dirty state needing intervention
   ```bash
   bd list --label polecat:<name> --status=open
   ```
   Process in process-cleanups step.

3. **No wisp, polecat exists** - POLECAT_DONE mail was missed
   Check for pending MR before taking any action:
   ```bash
   # Check for pending MR (gt-6a9d: do NOT nuke if MR pending)
   bd list --label polecat:<name>,state:merge-requested --status=open
   # If no pending MR and no dirty state → polecat is idle, leave it
   ```
   If dirty state exists, create cleanup wisp for investigation.

**Step 5: Execute nudges**
```bash
# Use --mode=queue to avoid interrupting in-flight tool calls
gt nudge --mode=queue <rig>/polecats/<name> "How's progress? Need help?"
```

**Step 6: Escalate if needed**
```bash
gt mail send deacon/ -s "Escalation: <polecat> stuck" \
  -m "Polecat <name> reports stuck. Please intervene."
```

**Parallelism**: Use Task tool subagents to inspect multiple polecats concurrently.

**ZFC Principle**: Trust agent_state from beads for WHAT agents report. But
verify tmux session existence for WHETHER agents are alive. A dead session with
agent_state=running is a zombie — the agent cannot correct its own state.

**Step 7: ORPHANED BEAD DETECTION — Scan from beads side**

🚨 **CRITICAL**: Zombie detection (Step 2a) scans FROM polecat directories.
Once a polecat is nuked and its directory removed, its beads become invisible
to zombie detection. Orphaned bead detection scans FROM beads to catch this case.

```bash
bd list --status=in_progress --json --limit=0
bd list --status=hooked --json --limit=0
```

For each in_progress or hooked bead with a polecat assignee (format: `<rig>/polecats/<name>`):
0. Verify bead status is still in_progress/hooked (not closed since listing). If
   closed, skip — the polecat completed its work. (gt-sy8)
1. Only check beads assigned to polecats in YOUR rig
2. Check tmux session: `gt session status <rig>/<name> --json | jq -r '.running'`
3. Check polecat directory: `ls <rig>/polecats/<name> 2>/dev/null`
4. If BOTH session dead AND directory missing → orphan. Reset the bead:
   ```bash
   bd update <bead-id> --status=open --assignee=
   gt mail send deacon/ -s "ORPHAN_RECOVERED: <bead-id>" \
     -m "Bead <bead-id> was assigned to <rig>/polecats/<name> which no longer exists.
   The bead has been reset to open with no assignee.
   Please re-dispatch to an available polecat."
   ```
5. If directory exists but session dead → skip (zombie detection handles it)
6. If session alive → not an orphan, skip"
+description = "Survey all polecats using agent beads and tmux session cross-reference.

## PRIMARY: Discover completions from agent bead metadata (gt-w0br)

Before zombie detection or progress checks, scan agent beads for completion
metadata written by `gt done`. This is the PRIMARY mechanism for discovering
polecat state transitions. The inbox-check POLECAT_DONE mail is now fallback only.

Completion metadata fields on agent beads (set by gt done):
- `exit_type`: COMPLETED, ESCALATED, DEFERRED, PHASE_COMPLETE
- `mr_id`: MR bead ID (if MR was created)
- `branch`: Working branch name
- `mr_failed`: true if MR creation failed
- `completion_time`: RFC3339 timestamp

**Step 0: Discover completions from beads**

The `DiscoverCompletions()` function (witness/handlers.go) handles this:
1. Scans all polecat agent beads for `exit_type` + `completion_time` set
2. Routes each: MR present → cleanup wisp + MERGE_READY; no MR → acknowledge idle
3. Clears completion metadata after processing (prevents re-processing)

This replaces the reactive POLECAT_DONE mail flow with proactive bead discovery.

🚨 **SWIM LANE RULE: You may ONLY close wisps that YOU (the witness) created.**
Do NOT close formula wisps, polecat work wisps, or any wisp created by `gt sling`
or another agent. Wisp lifecycle for non-witness wisps is the reaper Dog's job.
If you encounter wisps that look orphaned but weren't created by your patrol,
report them to Deacon — do NOT close them. Closing foreign wisps kills active
polecat work molecules.

**Step 1: List polecat agent beads**

```bash
bd list --type=agent --json
```

Filter the JSON output for entries where description contains `role_type: polecat`.
Each polecat agent bead has fields in its description:
- `role_type: polecat`
- `rig: <rig-name>`
- `agent_state: running|idle|awaiting_verdict|stuck|done`
- `hook_bead: <current-work-id>`

**Step 2: For each polecat, check agent_state**

| agent_state | Meaning | Action |
|-------------|---------|--------|
| running | Actively working | Check for zombie (Step 2a), then progress (Step 3) |
| working | Actively working | Check for zombie (Step 2a), then progress (Step 3) |
| awaiting_verdict | MR submitted, waiting for refinery | Check for zombie (Step 2c) |
| spawning | Agent initializing | Skip zombie detection. Check spawn age (Step 2b) |
| idle | No work assigned | Leave alone — sandbox preserved for reuse (Step 3a) |
| stuck | Self-reported stuck | Handle stuck protocol |
| done | Work complete | Verify cleanup triggered (see Step 4a) |

**Step 2a: ZOMBIE DETECTION — Cross-reference tmux session existence**

🚨 **CRITICAL**: Zombies cannot send signals. A polecat with agent_state=running
or hook_bead assigned but NO tmux session is a zombie that will sit forever
undetected unless you proactively check.

⚠️ **SKIP spawning polecats**: Polecats with agent_state=spawning are still
initializing (worktree creation, dependency install, tmux session startup).
They will NOT have a tmux session yet — this is expected, not a zombie.
Do NOT run zombie detection on spawning polecats. Handle them in Step 2b instead.

For EVERY polecat with agent_state=running/working (NOT spawning/awaiting_verdict) OR hook_bead assigned with non-spawning state:
(awaiting_verdict polecats have their own zombie detection in Step 2c)
```bash
gt session status <rig>/<name> --json | jq -r '.running' | grep -q true && echo ALIVE || echo ZOMBIE
```

**If ZOMBIE detected** (session missing, agent says working):

**IMPORTANT (gt-sy8)**: Before processing as zombie, check if the hook_bead is
already CLOSED:
```bash
bd show <hook_bead> --json | jq -r '.[0].status'
```
If status is "closed", the polecat completed its work successfully. The dead
session is expected (gt done kills it). Just nuke the dead session — do NOT
trigger re-dispatch or send RECOVERED_BEAD/RECOVERY_NEEDED to Deacon.

1. Check git state to determine if work is recoverable:
```bash
cd polecats/<name>/<rig>
git status --porcelain         # Uncommitted changes?
git log @{u}..HEAD      # Unpushed commits?
```

2. **If clean** (no uncommitted, no unpushed): Check for pending MR first.
```bash
# CRITICAL (gt-6a9d): Check for pending MR before any nuke!
bd list --label polecat:<name>,state:merge-requested --status=open
# If merge-requested wisp exists → DO NOT NUKE, MR pending in refinery
# If no pending MR → safe to nuke (zombie with no work to preserve)
gt session restart <rig>/<name>
```

3. **If dirty** (has unpushed/uncommitted work): Escalate to Deacon for recovery.
```bash
gt mail send deacon/ -s "RECOVERY_NEEDED <rig>/<name>" \
  -m "Polecat: <rig>/<name>
Cleanup Status: <has_uncommitted|has_unpushed|has_stash>
Hook Bead: <hook_bead>
Detected: $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)

Zombie detected: tmux session dead, agent_state=<state>.
This polecat has unpushed/uncommitted work that will be lost if nuked.
Please coordinate recovery before authorizing cleanup."
```

Also create a cleanup wisp for tracking:
```bash
bd create --ephemeral --title "cleanup:<name>" \
  --description "Zombie detected: session dead, state=<agent_state>" \
  --labels cleanup,polecat:<name>,state:zombie-detected
```

**Step 2b: STALE SPAWN DETECTION — Check spawn age for spawning polecats**

For polecats with agent_state=spawning, check how long they've been spawning.
Spawning should complete within 5 minutes even on large repos.

```bash
# Get the agent bead's updated_at timestamp to estimate spawn start
bd show <agent-bead> --json | jq -r '.[0].updated_at'
# Compare with current time
```

| Spawn age | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| < 5 min | Normal — leave alone, spawning in progress |
| 5-10 min | Warning — log observation, check again next cycle |
| > 10 min | Stale spawn — escalate (do NOT nuke) |

**If stale spawn detected** (spawning > 10 min):
```bash
gt escalate -s HIGH "Stale spawn: <rig>/<name> has been spawning for <N> minutes"
```

Do NOT nuke stale spawning polecats. The sling process may be slow (large repo
clone, dependency install) or stuck. Escalation lets a human or Mayor investigate
without destroying a potentially-in-progress setup.

**Step 2c: AWAITING_VERDICT ZOMBIE DETECTION**

Polecats in `awaiting_verdict` state have submitted their MR and are waiting for
the refinery to send MERGED or FIX_NEEDED. This is a valid long-running state.

⚠️ **Do NOT treat awaiting_verdict as idle or stuck.** The polecat is legitimately
waiting for an external signal. However, if the session dies while waiting, the
polecat becomes a zombie that will never receive the signal.

For EVERY polecat with agent_state=awaiting_verdict:
```bash
gt session status <rig>/<name> --json | jq -r '.running' | grep -q true && echo ALIVE || echo ZOMBIE
```

**If ALIVE**: Leave alone. The polecat is waiting for its verdict. No action needed.
Do NOT nudge awaiting_verdict polecats — they are correctly idle-waiting.

**If ZOMBIE** (session dead while awaiting_verdict):
The polecat died while waiting for the refinery verdict. Restart it so it can
re-check for pending FIX_NEEDED or MERGED signals:
```bash
# Check git state first
cd polecats/<name>/<rig>
git status --porcelain
```

If clean (expected for awaiting_verdict — work was already pushed):
```bash
gt session restart <rig>/<name>
```

If dirty (unexpected — should have been committed before submitting MR):
```bash
gt mail send deacon/ -s "RECOVERY_NEEDED <rig>/<name>" \
  -m "Polecat: <rig>/<name>
State: awaiting_verdict (zombie)
Hook Bead: <hook_bead>
Git status: dirty (unexpected for awaiting_verdict)

Zombie detected while awaiting refinery verdict.
Has uncommitted work that should have been pushed before MR submission.
Please coordinate recovery."
```

**Step 3: For running polecats (with LIVE session), assess progress**

Check the hook_bead field to see what they're working on:
```bash
bd show <hook_bead>  # See current step/issue
```

You can also verify they're responsive:
```bash
gt peek <rig>/<name> 20
```

Look for:
- Recent tool activity → making progress
- Idle at prompt → may need nudge
- Error messages → may need help

**Step 3a: For idle polecats, verify sandbox health**

When agent_state=idle, the polecat has no work assigned. Its sandbox is
preserved for reuse by future slings (persistent polecat model, gt-4ac).

⚠️ **Do NOT nuke idle polecats.** Their sandbox is preserved for reuse.
Nuking would force a full re-clone on the next sling, which is slow.

Check for pending MRs — an idle polecat may have work in the refinery:
```bash
# Check for cleanup wisps (merge-requested = MR pending in refinery)
bd list --label polecat:<name>,state:merge-requested --status=open
```
If a merge-requested wisp exists, the polecat's MR is in the refinery queue.
Do NOT nuke — the refinery needs the remote branch.

**If dirty** (uncommitted or unpushed work):
```bash
# Escalate to Deacon - polecat has work that might be valuable
gt mail send deacon/ -s \"IDLE_DIRTY: <polecat> has uncommitted work\" \
  -m \"Polecat: <name>
State: idle (no hook_bead)
Git status: <uncommitted-files>
Unpushed commits: <count>

Please advise: recover work or discard?\"
```

**Rationale**: Idle polecats are preserved for reuse. Their sandbox contains
a pre-configured worktree that saves clone time on the next sling. Only
escalate when there's actual dirty state at risk.

**Step 4: Decide action**

| Observation | Action |
|-------------|--------|
| agent_state=running, session alive, recent activity | None |
| agent_state=running, session alive, idle 5-15 min | Gentle nudge |
| agent_state=running, session alive, idle 15+ min | Direct nudge with deadline |
| agent_state=running, SESSION DEAD | ZOMBIE — handle in Step 2a |
| agent_state=awaiting_verdict, session alive | None — waiting for refinery verdict |
| agent_state=awaiting_verdict, SESSION DEAD | ZOMBIE — restart session (Step 2c) |
| agent_state=spawning, < 5 min | None — spawning in progress |
| agent_state=spawning, 5-10 min | Log warning, check next cycle |
| agent_state=spawning, > 10 min | Stale spawn — escalate (Step 2b) |
| agent_state=stuck | Assess and help or escalate |
| agent_state=done | Verify cleanup triggered (see Step 4a) |

**Step 4a: Handle agent_state=done**

In the persistent model, polecats with agent_state=done should be idle with
their sandbox preserved. Finding one here indicates:

1. **Stale agent bead** - polecat was nuked but bead remains
   ```bash
   # Verify polecat doesn't exist anymore
   ls polecats/<name> 2>/dev/null || echo "Already nuked"
   ```
   If nuked, the agent bead is stale. Clean it up or ignore.

2. **Cleanup wisp exists** - polecat has dirty state needing intervention
   ```bash
   bd list --label polecat:<name> --status=open
   ```
   Process in process-cleanups step.

3. **No wisp, polecat exists** - POLECAT_DONE mail was missed
   Check for pending MR before taking any action:
   ```bash
   # Check for pending MR (gt-6a9d: do NOT nuke if MR pending)
   bd list --label polecat:<name>,state:merge-requested --status=open
   # If no pending MR and no dirty state → polecat is idle, leave it
   ```
   If dirty state exists, create cleanup wisp for investigation.

**Step 5: Execute nudges**
```bash
# Use --mode=queue to avoid interrupting in-flight tool calls
gt nudge --mode=queue <rig>/polecats/<name> "How's progress? Need help?"
```

**Step 6: Escalate if needed**
```bash
gt mail send deacon/ -s "Escalation: <polecat> stuck" \
  -m "Polecat <name> reports stuck. Please intervene."
```

**Parallelism**: Use Task tool subagents to inspect multiple polecats concurrently.

**ZFC Principle**: Trust agent_state from beads for WHAT agents report. But
verify tmux session existence for WHETHER agents are alive. A dead session with
agent_state=running is a zombie — the agent cannot correct its own state.

**Step 7: ORPHANED BEAD DETECTION — Scan from beads side**

🚨 **CRITICAL**: Zombie detection (Step 2a) scans FROM polecat directories.
Once a polecat is nuked and its directory removed, its beads become invisible
to zombie detection. Orphaned bead detection scans FROM beads to catch this case.

```bash
bd list --status=in_progress --json --limit=0
bd list --status=hooked --json --limit=0
```

For each in_progress or hooked bead with a polecat assignee (format: `<rig>/polecats/<name>`):
0. Verify bead status is still in_progress/hooked (not closed since listing). If
   closed, skip — the polecat completed its work. (gt-sy8)
1. Only check beads assigned to polecats in YOUR rig
2. Check tmux session: `gt session status <rig>/<name> --json | jq -r '.running'`
3. Check polecat directory: `ls <rig>/polecats/<name> 2>/dev/null`
4. If BOTH session dead AND directory missing → orphan. Reset the bead:
   ```bash
   bd update <bead-id> --status=open --assignee=
   gt mail send deacon/ -s "ORPHAN_RECOVERED: <bead-id>" \
     -m "Bead <bead-id> was assigned to <rig>/polecats/<name> which no longer exists.
   The bead has been reset to open with no assignee.
   Please re-dispatch to an available polecat."
   ```
5. If directory exists but session dead → skip (zombie detection handles it)
6. If session alive → not an orphan, skip"
